content.problemStatement:
Flatland is a 1D country ‚ÄĒ all points in Flatland lie on one line. Everybody in Flatland loves pizza (because it's flat enough).

There are nnn pizzerias numbered 111 through nnn, which serve mmm consumers numbered 111 through mmm. Let's denote the position of the iii-th pizzeria by sis_isi‚Äč and the position of the iii-th consumer's home by cic_ici‚Äč. No two pizzerias are located at the same position, but the position of any consumer can coincide with the position of any pizzeria or consumer.

Every consumer wants to order one pizza, spending as little money as possible. The iii-th pizzeria sells each pizza at a certain baseline price pip_ipi‚Äč; delivering pizza from point x1x_1x1‚Äč to point x2x_2x2‚Äč costs an additional (x1‚ąíx2)2(x_1 - x_2) ^ 2(x1‚Äč‚ąíx2‚Äč)2. These prices are independent ‚ÄĒ even if multiple pizzas can be delivered at once, each consumer needs to pay the full price for their delivery.

Unfortunately, some consumers don't like some pizzerias, so they won't order pizza from them. Specifically, for each consumer, you are given a list of pizzerias this consumer won't order from no matter what.

For each consumer, find the amount of money they will spend for the pizza with delivery.
constraints: 0‚Č§si,ci‚Č§1090 \le s_i, c_i \le 10^90‚Č§si‚Äč,ci‚Äč‚Č§109 for each valid iii
0‚Č§ki‚Č§n‚ąí10 \le k_i \le n-10‚Č§ki‚Äč‚Č§n‚ąí1 for each valid iii
1‚Č§di,j‚Č§n1 \le d_{i, j} \le n1‚Č§di,j‚Äč‚Č§n for each valid i,ji, ji,j
s1,s2,‚Ä¶,sns_1, s_2, \dots, s_ns1‚Äč,s2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,sn‚Äč are pairwise distinct
**Subtask #2 (35 points):** ki=0k_i = 0ki‚Äč=0 for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers nnn and mmm denoting the number of pizzerias and the number of consumers.
For each iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§n1 \le i \le n1‚Č§i‚Č§n), the iii-th of the following nnn lines contains two space-separated integers sis_isi‚Äč and pip_ipi‚Äč.
For each iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§m1 \le i \le m1‚Č§i‚Č§m), the iii-th of the following mmm lines contains two space-separated integers cic_ici‚Äč and kik_iki‚Äč, followed by a space (if ki>0k_i \gt 0ki‚Äč>0) and kik_iki‚Äč space-separated integers di,1,di,2,‚Ä¶,di,kid_{i, 1}, d_{i, 2}, \dots, d_{i, k_i}di,1‚Äč,di,2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,di,ki‚Äč‚Äč denoting the list of pizzerias the iii-th consumer won't order from.
content.outputFormat: Print mmm lines. For each iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§m1 \le i \le m1‚Č§i‚Č§m), the iii-th of these lines should contain a single integer ‚ÄĒ the amount of money the iii-th consumer will spend.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The first consumer likes all the pizzerias, so they order a pizza from the first pizzeria, since it will cost the least, 7+(3‚ąí1)2=117 + (3 - 1)^2 = 117+(3‚ąí1)2=11.
The second consumer doesn't like the first pizzeria, so they won't order from there, even though it's the cheapest option. Therefore, they order a pizza from the third pizzeria. It costs 9+(8‚ąí3)2=349 + (8 - 3)^2 = 349+(8‚ąí3)2=34.
The third consumer likes only the third pizzeria, so they order from there. It costs 9+(8‚ąí6)2=139 + (8 - 6)^2 = 139+(8‚ąí6)2=13.
